content.content:
1	import type { NextConfig } from "next";
2	
3	// Hybrid build target:
4	//   - unset (default): regular Next.js server build supporting route handlers (/api/currents/*).
5	//   - "standalone":    containerized deploys (Docker/self-hosted).
6	//   - "export":        legacy fully-static export (route handlers will NOT work).
7	const buildTarget = process.env.NEXT_BUILD_TARGET;
8	
9	const nextConfig: NextConfig = {
10	  output:
11	    buildTarget === "standalone"
12	      ? "standalone"
13	      : buildTarget === "export"
14	        ? "export"
15	        : undefined,
16	  images: { unoptimized: true },
17	};
18	
19	export default nextConfig;
20

1	# Used for canonical URLs in the UI + feed generation (optional; defaults are safe for local builds).
2	# NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL="https://public.theseus.invalid"
3	
4	# If set, the /responses form POSTs to the Theseus Codex moderation queue.
5	# NEXT_PUBLIC_PORTAL_API="http://localhost:3000"
6	
7	# Used by `scripts/write-feeds.mjs` (optional; falls back to NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL).
8	# THESEUS_PUBLIC_SITE_URL="https://public.theseus.invalid"
9	
10	# URL of the FastAPI current_events_api service (server-side only, never exposed to browser).
11	CURRENTS_API_URL=http://127.0.0.1:8088
12	
13	# Optional: client-side base for fetches from currentsApi.ts (defaults to /api/currents if unset).
14	NEXT_PUBLIC_CURRENTS_PROXY_BASE=/api/currents
15	
16	# Build target for containerized deploys only; leave unset for Vercel / standard Next server.
17	#   standalone = self-contained Node server output (Docker)
18	#   export     = legacy fully-static export (disables /api/currents route handlers)
19	# NEXT_BUILD_TARGET=standalone
20

file.content:
# Used for canonical URLs in the UI + feed generation (optional; defaults are safe for local builds).
# NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL="https://public.theseus.invalid"

# If set, the /responses form POSTs to the Theseus Codex moderation queue.
# NEXT_PUBLIC_PORTAL_API="http://localhost:3000"

# Used by `scripts/write-feeds.mjs` (optional; falls back to NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL).
# THESEUS_PUBLIC_SITE_URL="https://public.theseus.invalid"

# URL of the FastAPI current_events_api service (server-side only, never exposed to browser).
CURRENTS_API_URL=http://127.0.0.1:8088

# Optional: client-side base for fetches from currentsApi.ts (defaults to /api/currents if unset).
NEXT_PUBLIC_CURRENTS_PROXY_BASE=/api/currents

# Build target for containerized deploys only; leave unset for Vercel / standard Next server.
#   standalone = self-contained Node server output (Docker)
#   export     = legacy fully-static export (disables /api/currents route handlers)
# NEXT_BUILD_TARGET=standalone

content.text:
Prompt 08 was already completed at commit `15ebc8a` and prompts 09–14 have since been built on top of it. No new work required — all scope items exist and match the spec, and the `currentsApi.test.ts` suite passes (6/6).

## Report-back

**Proxy origin env var:**
- `CURRENTS_API_URL` (server-side, default `http://127.0.0.1:8088`) — read by every route handler under `src/app/api/currents/**`, never exposed to the browser.
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_CURRENTS_PROXY_BASE` (client-side, default `/api/currents`) — used by `src/lib/currentsApi.ts` so the browser only ever talks to the Next proxy.

**Static vs. runtime pages:**
- **Static (prerendered at build):** `/` (`page.tsx`), `/c/[slug]`, `/c/[slug]/v/[version]`, `/conclusions/[id]`, `/interop`, `/interop/[mipName]/[mipVersion]`, `/methodology` (+ `/decay`, `/overrides`, `/rigor`), `/methods`, `/methods/[name]/[version]`, `/open-questions`, `/responses`. None of these declare `export const dynamic`; they keep their existing static behavior, and `scripts/write-feeds.mjs` still runs in `prebuild`.
- **Runtime (force-dynamic, Node runtime):** `/currents` and `/currents/[id]` (added in prompts 09–12) plus every route handler under `/api/currents/**` (list, detail, sources, follow-up POST SSE, follow-up messages, `stream`).

**Next config changes:**
- `next.config.ts` uses a `NEXT_BUILD_TARGET` env switch instead of hardcoding `output: "export"`. Default (unset) produces a regular Next server build that supports route handlers; `NEXT_BUILD_TARGET=standalone` targets Docker; `NEXT_BUILD_TARGET=export` preserves the legacy static export path (but disables `/api/currents`). `images.unoptimized: true` retained.

**Deploy-config note for prompt 16:** `netlify.toml` / `vercel.json` still assume the old `out/` static export. They need to either drop the static-publish directive (Vercel) or set `NEXT_BUILD_TARGET=export` *only* if we're willing to lose the proxy — the real fix is to switch those targets to the hybrid server build.

**Known unrelated build issue:** `npm run build` currently fails at the TypeScript step on `playwright.config.ts` (missing `@playwright/test` dep). This file is untracked and belongs to prompt 17 (e2e harness), not prompt 08; I did not touch it. Build succeeds through compile + `write-feeds` prebuild; only the top-level tsc pass trips on the playwright file. The `currentsApi.test.ts` vitest suite passes cleanly.

result:
Prompt 08 was already completed at commit `15ebc8a` and prompts 09–14 have since been built on top of it. No new work required — all scope items exist and match the spec, and the `currentsApi.test.ts` suite passes (6/6).

## Report-back

**Proxy origin env var:**
- `CURRENTS_API_URL` (server-side, default `http://127.0.0.1:8088`) — read by every route handler under `src/app/api/currents/**`, never exposed to the browser.
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_CURRENTS_PROXY_BASE` (client-side, default `/api/currents`) — used by `src/lib/currentsApi.ts` so the browser only ever talks to the Next proxy.

**Static vs. runtime pages:**
- **Static (prerendered at build):** `/` (`page.tsx`), `/c/[slug]`, `/c/[slug]/v/[version]`, `/conclusions/[id]`, `/interop`, `/interop/[mipName]/[mipVersion]`, `/methodology` (+ `/decay`, `/overrides`, `/rigor`), `/methods`, `/methods/[name]/[version]`, `/open-questions`, `/responses`. None of these declare `export const dynamic`; they keep their existing static behavior, and `scripts/write-feeds.mjs` still runs in `prebuild`.
- **Runtime (force-dynamic, Node runtime):** `/currents` and `/currents/[id]` (added in prompts 09–12) plus every route handler under `/api/currents/**` (list, detail, sources, follow-up POST SSE, follow-up messages, `stream`).

**Next config changes:**
- `next.config.ts` uses a `NEXT_BUILD_TARGET` env switch instead of hardcoding `output: "export"`. Default (unset) produces a regular Next server build that supports route handlers; `NEXT_BUILD_TARGET=standalone` targets Docker; `NEXT_BUILD_TARGET=export` preserves the legacy static export path (but disables `/api/currents`). `images.unoptimized: true` retained.

**Deploy-config note for prompt 16:** `netlify.toml` / `vercel.json` still assume the old `out/` static export. They need to either drop the static-publish directive (Vercel) or set `NEXT_BUILD_TARGET=export` *only* if we're willing to lose the proxy — the real fix is to switch those targets to the hybrid server build.

**Known unrelated build issue:** `npm run build` currently fails at the TypeScript step on `playwright.config.ts` (missing `@playwright/test` dep). This file is untracked and belongs to prompt 17 (e2e harness), not prompt 08; I did not touch it. Build succeeds through compile + `write-feeds` prebuild; only the top-level tsc pass trips on the playwright file. The `currentsApi.test.ts` vitest suite passes cleanly.
